title: Regulatory Reporting – Legal Entity Controller, Assistant Vice President
description:
Who we are looking for

State Street Corporation’s Finance division is currently seeking an AVP for Legal Entity Reporting mainly being responsible in the Regulatory Reporting Area. As Legal Entity Controller you will take responsibility of financial and regulatory reporting as well as monitoring of various legal entities within the ASEAN region. You will develop an in-depth understanding of various entities within the financial industry, including their business strategies as well as associated technical accounting and disclosure requirements. We are looking for a proactive and self-motivated team player to join our Legal Entity Controller team based in Singapore who is willing to take over responsibilities in a wide range of accounting and reporting requirements in a fast-paced banking environment.

Why this role is important to us

The team you will be joining is a part of State Street Global Services (SSGS). SSGS gives asset owners and managers access to the essential financial tools they need to deliver effective investment solutions. From core custody, accounting, fund administration and shareholder recordkeeping, to complete operations solutions and servicing for alternative assets like OTC derivatives, private equity and real estate, SSGS’ tools help our clients make better investment choices and act on growth opportunities.

Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for.

What you will be responsible for

As Regulatory Reporting – Legal Entity Controller you will

  • Reporting into Regional ASEAN Controller.

  • Supporting various aspects of legal entity and BU Controllership within the ASEAN region (spanning Singapore, Thailand, Malaysia, and Brunei) to ensure accuracy of financial results and that all internal controls are appropriately designed and executed. Coverage for Global Services, Global Delivery, Global Markets, Global Exchange and Global Advisors in region for Controllership impacts.

  • Regulatory reporting to MAS and implementation of the new MAS 610 framework - Ensure all regulatory returns, statistics and surveys data are prepared accurately and timely, and in compliance with regulatory and statutory requirements which includes maintain and write up of respective procedures and documentation.

  • Collaborate within a small team based out of Singapore and partnering with a wide range of stakeholders including Businesses, the local management team, Lead Financial Officers (LFOs), outsourced functions team (Global Accounting Operations, Billing, Regulatory Reporting) and Finance /functional leadership team. Working closely with Business Controls teams to improve control environment.

  • Coordinate with Global Close and Consolidation team on monthly close cycle process for accuracy and efficiency. Ensure financial controls are operating effectively and issues reported/ resolved and appropriately escalated. Responsibility for Internal Audit results and remediation for Singapore entities.

  • Actively cooperate with the external auditors to finalize statutory reporting for various entities in the ASEAN region advisor including query resolution.

  • Interface and close collaboration with LFO’s and FP&A organization to ensure alignment and accuracy or monthly, Quarterly/ Annual results and alignment to Business Unit initiatives and correct treatment of key events (Impairment, deferrals etc).

  • Support and execution of transformation initiatives, ensure robust control and knowledge transfer for activities migrated to Hub Locations, proper implementation of systems which impact the external & internal financial & regulatory reporting in the ASEAN region. Execute on initiatives that impact accounting, reporting or tax including capital management

  • Support tax compliance (direct and Indirect) including Transfer Pricing scheme.

  • Any other ad-hoc tasks as required by the APAC controller from time-to-time.

What we value

These skills will help you succeed in this role

  • Ability to work in a fast paced banking environment managing multiple priorities

  • Fast learner, proactive in nature and has excellent attention for details

  • Committed, self-motivated and proactive, with a desire to succeed

  • Team player

  • Ability to think holistically and make connections between diverse issues/tasks

Education & Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with local banking MAS (610, 649) reporting requirements will be required.

  • Qualified Accountant with approximately 10 years of relevant experience.

  • Good knowledge of Singapore GAAP / IFRS preferred

  • Good knowledge of Accounting System (e.g. Oracle, SUN) will be an advantage

  • Excellent written and oral communication skills in English required

  • Strong control awareness

  • Proficient level of Excel knowledge (e.g. Macro-handling) will be an advantage

postedCompany.description:
STATE STREET

State Street Corporation (NYSE: STT) is the world's leading provider of financial services to institutional investors including investment servicing, investment management and investment research and trading. With $27.48 trillion in assets under custody and administration and $2.38 trillion in assets under management as of March 31, 2014, State Street operates globally in more than 100 geographic markets and employs 29,530 worldwide.

 

For more information, visit State Street's website at www.statestreet.com. State Street is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and prohibits discrimination against applicants or employees on the basis of any legally protected characteristic.

Please submit an application to the State Street Careers site:   www.statestreet.com/careers  and quote the job ID number of this role.
